David was anointed king a third time. His progress was gradual, so that his faith might be tested and he could gain experience. In this way, his kingdom showed what was to come for the Messiah, which would increase gradually. Thus Jesus became our Brother, took on our nature, lived in it so that he could become our Prince and Savior: in this way, a humbled sinner finds encouragement in this close relationship, asks for salvation, submits to his authority, and seeks his protection.

The enemies of God’s people are often very confident of their own strength and most secure when their time to fall is near. But the pride and arrogance of the Jebusites encouraged David, and the Lord God of hosts was with him. In this way, on the day of God's power, Satan's stronghold, the human heart, becomes a dwelling place for God through the Spirit, and a throne where the Son of David rules, bringing every thought into obedience to himself. May he come in this way, claim us, and cleanse each of our hearts; and, destroying every idol, may he reign there forever!

David’s house was not made worse or less suitable for dedication to God because it was built by the sons of foreigners. It is prophesied about the gospel church: The sons of strangers will build up your walls, and their kings will serve you. David's government was established and built up. David was established as king; so is the Son of David, and all who, through him, are made kings and priests to our God. Never had the nation of Israel appeared so great as it began to be now. Many have God’s favor and love, but do not realize it, and therefore lack its comfort; but to be exalted to that, and to recognize it, is happiness. David acknowledged that God had done great things for him for the sake of his people, so that he could be a blessing to them, and they could be happy under him.

The Philistines did not consider that David had the presence of God with him, which Saul had forfeited and lost. The kingdom of the Messiah, as soon as it was established in the world, was attacked in this way by the powers of darkness. The Gentiles raged, and the kings of the earth set themselves to oppose it; but all in vain, etc. The destruction will turn back, as it did then, on Satan's own kingdom. David acknowledged his dependence on God for victory and referred himself to God’s good pleasure: Will you do it? The assurance that God has given us of victory over our spiritual enemies should encourage us in our spiritual conflicts. David waited until God moved; he acted then, but not before. He was trained up in dependence on God and his providence. God fulfilled his promise, and David did not fail to take advantage of his opportunities. When the kingdom of the Messiah was to be set up, the apostles, who were to defeat Satan's kingdom, must not attempt anything until they received the promise of the Spirit; who came with a sound from heaven, as of a rushing, mighty wind.
